items.title: 'We need all our numbers': Liberal rules out SA upper house presidency bid

children.content: Labor's path to getting legislation through the upper house may get slightly more difficult after Liberal MLC Dennis Hood — who was strongly linked to the upper house presidency role — said he would remain on the floor of parliament.

gallery.altText: An MP stands in a corridor of parliament.

thumbnail.altText: An MP stands in a corridor of parliament.

items.title: The alternative classroom disengaged students are choosing to be in

children.content: Of the students who have attended the Alternative Suspension program, 67 per cent have received no further suspensions.

image.altText: Art work of a young male student sitting at his desk in a classroom distracted

items.title: Crows claim epic after two goals inside final minute as Freo downs Dogs

children.content: Adelaide claims a remarkable one-point victory over Port Adelaide after Hayden Young fires the Dockers to victory over the Bulldogs.

image.altText: A group of AFL players celebrate a victory in front of crowd

items.title: 'Here we go again': Pirie locals concerned over uncertainty of smelter funding

children.content: A $135 million dollar assistance package for Nyrstar smelters in Port Pirie and Hobart ran out on Friday, with negotiations between the company and the South Australian, Tasmanian and federal governments continuing on a new deal.

articles.title: Smelter jobs at stake as government assistance runs dry

children.content: South Australia's premier says there is a "national imperative" to keep smelters running, as a $135 million assistance package for Nyrstar operations at Port Pirie and Hobart expires.

image.altText: An aerial view of the Nyrstar smelter in Port Pirie

gallery.altText: The sign for Ellen Street in the foreground with a large smoke stack from the lead smelter in the background

thumbnail.altText: The sign for Ellen Street in the foreground with a large smoke stack from the lead smelter in the background

items.title: Police confirm identity of remains found in SA bushland

children.content: SA Police say remains found in bushland south of Adelaide a week ago have been identified as belonging to Jeff Mundy, who was last seen by his family five years ago.

image.altText: Two women and a man stand near a fence with a bouquet of flowers attached to it, with trees behind

items.title: From near-misses and cancer to Olympic success, Glaetzer bows out

children.content: Matt Glaetzer was, for so long, the nearly man of cycling, heartbreakingly missing out on an elusive Olympic medal despite reaching five finals. But the South Australian refused to be beaten, either by that, or by cancer.

image.altText: Matt Glaetzer smiles and clenches his fist

items.title: SA government needs certainty on LIV Golf by October, premier says

children.content: The SA Premier says the government is "monitoring" the situation regarding LIV Golf closely, and would need a guarantee of its future in Adelaide by October.

articles.title: LIV Golf is falling apart on a flawed premise and a broken promise

children.content: While Adelaide showed just how good LIV Golf could be, the flawed premise of the breakaway tournament always spelled trouble.

image.altText: GettyImages-2147098984

articles.title: Aussie one of several big names left in the lurch as LIV Golf funding pulled

children.content: Australia's Cameron Smith is one of several big names in golf left with an uncertain future after Saudi Arabia pulled funding of LIV Golf.

image.altText: A golfer tees off.

gallery.altText: Three men smiling and walking, two of them in sportswear and one in a business suit

thumbnail.altText: Three men smiling and walking, two of them in sportswear and one in a business suit

items.title: 'Children have ended up on main roads', SA childcare regulator says

children.content: South Australia’s childcare centre watchdog says children have injured themselves or "ended up on main roads" due to poor supervision and that one in five services in the state do not meet national quality standards.

image.altText: A man in a dark blue suit and tie is sitting and looking ahead

items.title: Remembering SA's pain and battles behind the Murray-Darling Basin Plan

children.content: The Murray-Darling Basin Plan is under review, but with submissions due to close today, we take a step back in time to remember when dried lake beds were turning acidic, dairy farmers were selling up, and there was talk of water carting for Adelaide.

image.altText: Close up of cracks in dried lakebed

items.title: The algal bloom may be 'gone', but many are still grappling with the fall-out

children.content: While testing has shown a dramatic improvement in South Australia's algal bloom situation, industry and experts fear it won't be a quick recovery.

image.altText: A mural of a squid painted on the wall of a tackle shop.

items.title: Police charge man for allegedly attending businesses while nude

children.content: A Mount Gambier man is granted bail after he was allegedly nude while in businesses and on a busy main road three times in the past week.

image.altText: A police officer in a cap standing in front of a police station

items.title: ATSB confirms student and instructor killed in Parafield plane crash

children.content: The ATSB said the plane had been doing circuit training at the airport, but it's not yet known if structured training was underway when it crashed.

image.altText: Black smoke billows from a plane crash at Parafield Airport

items.title: Gas vs beer: How Pocock caused the government a tax headache

children.content: David Pocock was struggling to find cut-through on the issue of gas taxes, but then a question about the beer excise sparked a social media war of words.

image.altText: Senator David Pocock sits at a table with a neutral expression on his face.

items.title: What is a town camp and what is life like there for Aboriginal people?

children.content: Kumanjayi Little Baby is believed to be taken from her bed in the night while visiting a town camp near Alice Springs with her mother. This is how town camps came to be and what it is like to live in one.

articles.title: Kumanjayi Little Baby's family calls for calm following Alice riots

children.content: The family of Alice Springs five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby have called for calm following rioting in the outback town overnight, while police say her alleged murderer was beaten unconscious.

image.altText: A man stands inside a house.

gallery.altText: A drone shot of saltbush and roads in Alice Springs

thumbnail.altText: A drone shot of saltbush and roads in Alice Springs

items.title: Fiji considers state of emergency after suspected drug kingpin murder in custody

children.content: The suspected murder of a Fijian underworld figure held in military custody has sparked a new chapter in the island nation's wrestle with drug-related crimes, with the police and military combining forces for a highly visible "crackdown".

image.altText: Men in military uniforms search a man who is leaning on a car.

items.title: The four women who left 'Australia Street' to try and leave Syria

children.content: Four Australian women, their children and grandchildren are stuck in limbo in Syria after leaving a refugee camp for family members of suspected Islamic State fighters. This is what we know about them.

image.altText: A group of women wearing hijabs and face masks, accompanied by a group of children.

items.title: Water restrictions loophole has some big players still pumping, expert warns

children.content: A loophole in the NSW government's water restrictions in the northern basin of the Murray Darling means river water is still being sucked up by a handful of major licence holders.

image.altText: Menindee Lakes main weir.

items.title: Tie property tax changes to income tax cuts, productivity chief says

children.content: Productivity Commission chair Danielle Wood says there is a "case" for property tax changes, but for it to be serious reform, the economic benefit must flow to workers.

image.altText: A sold sticker on a real estate listing board.

items.title: Rare quadruplet birth stuns mum, 45, and doctors

children.content: A Kalgoorlie couple expecting triplets welcome a surprise fourth baby — delivering an extraordinarily rare set of quadruplets that stunned even their doctors.

items.title: Why many Australians are heading for this island in East Asia

children.content: Lots of Australians travel to Taiwan chasing flavour and wanting to try the best beef noodle soup — but they leave wanting to explore more of its history and nature.

items.title: A guide to the six books vying for the $60,000 Stella Prize

children.content: A memoir by Geraldine Brooks and a historical novel by actor-turned-author Tasma Walton are among the eclectic works up for one of Australia's most prestigious literary awards.
